summary: As of mid-2026 the yield/vaults/structured-products landscape is led by a new generation of protocols built around yield tokenization and synthetic-dollar yield rather than the auto-compounding aggregators that dominated 2021. Pendle is the clear category leader on TVL among yield-specific protocols (~$1.18B on DefiLlama's narrowed 'Yield' category page, though aggregate Pendle TVL has been reported far higher, with a 2025 all-time peak of ~$13.4B). Ethena's USDe synthetic dollar (~$5.5-6B supply in Q2 2026) sits adjacent to the category as the largest crypto-collateralized synthetic-dollar/structured-yield product. The 'curated vault' model led by Morpho Vaults (peaked >$10B TVL in Q4 2025, ~$5.8B after a Feb 2026 pullback) has absorbed institutional capital that once flowed to simple aggregators. Legacy aggregators Yearn (~$151M) and Beefy (~$110-186M) have shrunk dramatically from their 2021 peaks, while Convex (~$1.28B) remains the dominant veCRV vote-aggregator in the now much-smaller Curve ecosystem. The dominant 2024-2025 growth engine was points/airdrop-driven and structured yield: Ethena seasons + Pendle PT/YT markets + curator vaults.

leaders.name: Pendle
leaders.chain: Ethereum (primary), plus Arbitrum, Plasma, Hyperliquid L1, BSC, Base, Sonic, Mantle, Avalanche, OP, Berachain
leaders.value: ~$1.18B (DefiLlama 'Yield' category protocol page); aggregate Pendle TVL reported much higher across feeds; 2025 all-time peak ~$13.4B
leaders.note: Dominant yield-tokenization protocol (splits yield-bearing assets into PT principal tokens + YT yield tokens). Growth driven by Ethena sUSDe pools, weETH/LRT, RWA (USDY, USYC) and Aave PT-collateral listings. Boros (perp-funding/fixed-income venue) launched 2025. Average 2025 TVL ~$5.8B (+79% YoY); trading volume ~$47.8B.

leaders.name: Ethena (USDe / sUSDe)
leaders.chain: Ethereum (primary), BNB Chain, Arbitrum, Solana and other EVM chains via LayerZero OFT
leaders.value: ~$5.5-6B USDe supply (Q2 2026 per DefiLlama); historically peaked higher in 2024 bull run
leaders.note: Largest crypto-collateralized synthetic dollar after Sky's USDS. Delta-neutral basis trade (long staked ETH/LST, short ETH perps); sUSDe yield from funding + staking. sUSDe APY ranged from low single digits to >30% since 2024 launch, mostly 5-15%. The single biggest points/airdrop-season yield engine of 2024-2025; deeply integrated with Pendle and Aave.

leaders.name: Morpho (Morpho Vaults)
leaders.chain: Ethereum (primary), Base and other EVM chains
leaders.value: Peaked >$10B in Q4 2025; ~$5.8B as of late Feb 2026 after market pullback
leaders.note: Curated-vault leader bridging lending and structured yield. Morpho Blue (immutable primitive) + Morpho Vaults (curated allocation layer run by curators Steakhouse, Gauntlet, MEV Capital, Block Analitica). Drove institutional entry: Coinbase USDC lending, Apollo (up to 9% of MORPHO supply over 4 years), Bitwise institutional vaults. USDC vault rates ~4-8% in 2026. Gauntlet alone curates ~$1.5B.

leaders.name: Convex Finance
leaders.note: Yield optimizer / vote-aggregator for Curve. Routes user CRV into permanent locks for cvxCRV, making it the largest single veCRV holder (~40%+) and the 'kingmaker' of Curve emissions. Convex + Yearn wallets accounted for ~90% of votes in a Dec 2025 Curve DAO vote. TVL collapsed alongside Curve (Curve ~$1.5-2.6B vs 2022 peak >$24B).

leaders.name: Yearn Finance
leaders.note: Original DeFi yield aggregator / vaults pioneer and the 2021 category leader (>$7B peak). Auto-compounding vault strategies. Now a fraction of its peak after the 2022 bear market and migration of capital to curated-vault and yield-tokenization models. Still an active veCRV/Curve governance participant alongside Convex.

leaders.name: Beefy
leaders.note: Multichain auto-compounding yield optimizer; default venue for farming on smaller/newer chains thanks to ~40-chain coverage. Native token BIFI. Like Yearn, a much smaller share of the category than at its peak; serves users wanting simple auto-compounding without picking a curator.

leaders.name: Euler (Euler Vaults / EVK)
leaders.value: Re-emerged as a curated-vault competitor to Morpho in 2025-2026 (specific figure not independently confirmed here)
leaders.note: After its 2023 exploit and recovery, Euler relaunched with the Euler Vault Kit (EVK) as modular, curator-driven vaults, competing in the same curated-vault / structured-yield niche as Morpho. Figure left unquantified to avoid inventing a number.

leaders.name: CIAN (Yield Layer)
leaders.note: Leader of the pure 'yield-aggregator' DefiLlama sub-category in 2026, ahead of Yearn and Beefy. Illustrates how the aggregator label shifted toward newer automated/structured-yield layers as legacy aggregators shrank.

leadership_shift: 2021: Yearn was the flagship yield aggregator (>$7B TVL peak Dec 2021); the Curve Wars produced Convex, which rocketed past $20B TVL by early 2022 to become DeFi's #2 protocol as the dominant veCRV vote-aggregator. Beefy led multichain auto-compounding. 2022-2023: The bear market and Curve/Convex's contraction gutted aggregator TVL (Yearn fell from >$7B toward ~$1B and below; Convex shrank with Curve from >$24B to low single-digit billions). Simple auto-compounding lost relevance as a growth story. 2024-2025: Leadership decisively shifted to (a) yield tokenization, where Pendle became the category leader on the back of Ethena sUSDe, LRTs and RWAs, peaking ~$13.4B TVL in 2025; (b) synthetic-dollar/structured yield, where Ethena's USDe scaled into the multi-billion range and became the single biggest points/airdrop-driven yield engine; and (c) curated vaults, where Morpho Vaults (and a recovered Euler with its EVK) crossed >$10B and pulled in institutional capital (Coinbase, Apollo, Bitwise). 2026 (now): Pendle leads yield-specific TVL and Ethena leads synthetic-dollar yield; curated vaults (Morpho/Euler) are the institutional venue; Convex remains the Curve kingmaker but in a much smaller pond; Yearn and Beefy persist as legacy auto-compounders at a fraction of former size. The throughline: capital moved from auto-compounding aggregators -> vote-aggregation (Curve Wars) -> yield tokenization + synthetic-dollar yield + curator-run vaults, with points/airdrop incentives the primary 2024-2025 demand driver.
